title: Waymo’s driverless cars crash less often than people
excerpt: Waymo’s driverless vehicles have crash rates that are 68% lower than human drivers, new IIHS research shows. But as the technology is deployed more widely, a better system for tracking safety is needed.
content_html:
Waymo’s driverless vehicles have crash rates that are 68% lower than human drivers, new IIHS research shows. But as the technology is deployed more widely, a better system for tracking safety is needed.

July 23, 2026

Waymo’s driverless cars are involved in fewer crashes than human drivers, but the federal system for monitoring automated vehicles needs improvement, a new study from the Insurance Institute for Highway Safety shows.

Per vehicle mile traveled (VMT), Waymo’s driverless vehicles deployed in San Francisco, Phoenix, Los Angeles and Austin were involved in 68% fewer crashes than human drivers, researchers found. The sample was restricted to crashes that a person would typically report to the police.

“The results show that, on a limited scale, these driverless cars are safer than human drivers — who can be impaired or drowsy or suffer lapses in attention,” IIHS President David Harkey said. “However, the present data collection system isn’t good enough to allow continuous monitoring of a large-scale expansion.”

Started in 2009 as the Google self-driving car project, Waymo launched its driverless robotaxi service for the public in Phoenix in 2020, expanded to San Francisco in 2022 and Los Angeles in 2023, and began limited operation in Austin in 2024. Its vehicles are classified as Level 4 on the automation scale created by SAE International, which means the automation can do all the driving with no human supervision under certain conditions.

In contrast, the Level 2 partial automation systems that are widely available for purchase by the general public, such as General Motors’ SuperCruise or Ford’s BlueCruise, require a human driver to remain alert and ready to take over at all times. Those systems involve different risks: The monotony of supervision can lead to attention fatigue, and many drivers put too much trust in the technology.

Level 3 systems can operate without constant human supervision in specified conditions, but a human still needs to be in the driver seat and ready to intervene when requested. Vehicles with such systems are very rare. Level 5 vehicles, capable of driving without human input under all conditions, do not yet exist. 

Since 2014, California has required companies testing self-driving cars on public roads to report crash involvements that result in fatalities, injuries or property damage of any amount. The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) began requiring similar reporting in 2021 under a Standing General Order (SGO) on crash reporting for incidents involving automated driving systems. 

While the SGO database was primarily intended to track defects and enforce recalls, it is the only national data collection tool for crashes involving self-driving vehicles. For this reason, it is a key resource for safety research. However, neither it nor the California database makes it easy to compare the crash rates of Level 4 vehicles with those of human drivers.

While companies are required to report crashes, they are not obligated to report the miles they log or whether such miles involved a human driver. Waymo provides driverless VMT information voluntarily, but other companies do not. That makes it impossible to know how common it is for their vehicles to crash.

Even with the mileage data that Waymo provides, a direct comparison with the crash rates of humans is impossible without time-consuming operations to account for the differences between the reports required by the SGO and what humans report, the IIHS researchers found.

Human drivers are generally only required to report crashes that cause an injury or result in more than $1,000 in property damage to the police, though the damage threshold varies by state. Many drivers also opt not to report crashes that meet the reporting threshold to avoid an increase in their insurance premiums or for other reasons. Around half of all crashes and a third of injury crashes go unreported.

In contrast, until an amendment to the SGO that took effect in 2025, self-driving vehicle companies were required to log minor incidents as crashes. For example, a vehicle scraping its undercarriage as it turned into a parking lot would count as a crash. Though the property-damage thresholds are now more closely aligned, companies are still more likely than regular people to report all qualifying crashes due to the business risks of failing to comply with the regulation. Moreover, driverless vehicles require expensive sensors that can be damaged by minor impacts, making their crashes more likely to exceed the damage threshold.

For a realistic comparison of crash rates, therefore, IIHS researchers had to clean up the data from crash reports submitted to NHTSA by Cruise, Waymo, Zoox and others from 2021 to 2024. The first step was to cull hundreds of redundant records. They also needed to eliminate certain incidents. These included situations in which the automation wasn’t engaged, the vehicle wasn’t on a public road, or no real crash took place. This process eliminated about a quarter of the reported crashes.

The next step was to determine whether the incident was severe enough that a reasonable person would have reported it to the police. The researchers made this determination based on the nature of the impact and property damage, the types of injuries that were reported and other factors. For example, if a person complained of pain but left the scene, the researchers coded that crash as unlikely to be reported to the police. If an airbag deployed, they coded that crash as police-reportable due to the higher impact speed and the cost of airbag replacement — regardless of other property damage or injury.

Using that common-sense standard, they estimated that only 22% of the 736 public-road crashes in which the automation was engaged were likely police-reportable — 89 crashes involving Waymo vehicles, 50 involving Cruise vehicles, 10 involving Zoox vehicles and 10 involving other companies. Because mileage numbers were only available for Waymo, they used its crashes to calculate self-driving vehicle crash rates. Of Waymo’s 89 police-reportable crash involvements, 64 were in driverless operation, and the others involved supervised use of the automation.

Waymo vehicles traveled about 50 million miles in driverless operation over the study period, compared with about 222 billion miles by human drivers over the same period and in the same locations. Overall, Waymo’s crash involvement rate was 68% lower than that of human drivers — 76% lower in Phoenix, 35% lower in San Francisco, 71% lower in Los Angeles, and 4% higher in Austin, where the sample size was relatively small. Waymo vehicles were involved in 85% fewer single-vehicle crashes and 81% fewer injury crashes per VMT than human drivers.

“Those are encouraging signs for the future of driverless vehicles,” said Eric Teoh, IIHS director of statistical services and lead author of the study. “Now we need to get the data collection system right, so that we can ensure that level of safety continues as these technologies become more prevalent.”

sentences.en: Waymo’s driverless vehicles have crash rates that are 68% lower than human drivers, new IIHS research shows.
sentences.zh: 新的 IIHS 研究显示，Waymo 的无人驾驶车辆的碰撞率比人类驾驶员低 68%。

sentences.en: But as the technology is deployed more widely, a better system for tracking safety is needed.
sentences.zh: 但随着该技术的部署更加广泛，需要更好的安全跟踪系统。

blocks.html_en: Waymo’s driverless cars are involved in fewer crashes than human drivers, but the federal system for monitoring automated vehicles needs improvement, a new study from the Insurance Institute for Highway Safety shows.

sentences.en: Waymo’s driverless cars are involved in fewer crashes than human drivers, but the federal system for monitoring automated vehicles needs improvement, a new study from the Insurance Institute for Highway Safety shows.
sentences.zh: 公路安全保险研究所的一项新研究显示，Waymo 的无人驾驶汽车发生的事故比人类驾驶员少，但监控自动驾驶汽车的联邦系统需要改进。

blocks.html_en: Per vehicle mile traveled (VMT), Waymo’s driverless vehicles deployed in San Francisco, Phoenix, Los Angeles and Austin were involved in 68% fewer crashes than human drivers, researchers found. The sample was restricted to crashes that a person would typically report to the police.

sentences.en: Per vehicle mile traveled (VMT), Waymo’s driverless vehicles deployed in San Francisco, Phoenix, Los Angeles and Austin were involved in 68% fewer crashes than human drivers, researchers found.
sentences.zh: 研究人员发现，按照每车辆行驶里程 (VMT) 计算，Waymo 在旧金山、菲尼克斯、洛杉矶和奥斯汀部署的无人驾驶汽车发生的事故比人类驾驶员少 68%。

sentences.en: The sample was restricted to crashes that a person would typically report to the police.
sentences.zh: 样本仅限于人们通常会向警方报告的事故。

blocks.html_en: “The results show that, on a limited scale, these driverless cars are safer than human drivers — who can be impaired or drowsy or suffer lapses in attention,” IIHS President David Harkey said. “However, the present data collection system isn’t good enough to allow continuous monitoring of a large-scale expansion.”

sentences.en: “The results show that, on a limited scale, these driverless cars are safer than human drivers — who can be impaired or drowsy or suffer lapses in attention,” IIHS President David Harkey said.
sentences.zh: IIHS 总裁戴维·哈基 (David Harkey) 表示：“结果表明，在有限的范围内，这些无人驾驶汽车比人类驾驶员更安全——人类驾驶员可能会受到损害、昏昏欲睡或注意力不集中。”

sentences.en: “However, the present data collection system isn’t good enough to allow continuous monitoring of a large-scale expansion.”
sentences.zh: “然而，目前的数据收集系统还不够好，无法持续监控大规模扩张。”

blocks.html_en: Started in 2009 as the Google self-driving car project, Waymo launched its driverless robotaxi service for the public in Phoenix in 2020, expanded to San Francisco in 2022 and Los Angeles in 2023, and began limited operation in Austin in 2024. Its vehicles are classified as Level 4 on the automation scale created by SAE International, which means the automation can do all the driving with no human supervision under certain conditions.

sentences.en: Its vehicles are classified as Level 4 on the automation scale created by SAE International, which means the automation can do all the driving with no human supervision under certain conditions.
sentences.zh: 其车辆在SAE国际制定的自动化等级中被列为4级，这意味着在一定条件下，自动化可以完成所有驾驶，无需人工监督。

sentences.zh: 相比之下，公众广泛购买的二级部分自动化系统，例如通用汽车的 SuperCruise 或福特的 BlueCruise，需要人类驾驶员随时保持警惕并准备好接管。

sentences.en: Those systems involve different risks: The monotony of supervision can lead to attention fatigue, and many drivers put too much trust in the technology.

sentences.en: Vehicles with such systems are very rare.
sentences.zh: 配备此类系统的车辆非常罕见。

sentences.en: Level 5 vehicles, capable of driving without human input under all conditions, do not yet exist.
sentences.zh: 能够在所有条件下无需人工操作即可驾驶的 5 级车辆尚不存在。

sentences.zh: 例如，如果一个人抱怨疼痛但离开了现场，研究人员会将这次事故编码为不太可能向警方报告。

sentences.en: If an airbag deployed, they coded that crash as police-reportable due to the higher impact speed and the cost of airbag replacement — regardless of other property damage or injury.
sentences.zh: 如果安全气囊展开，他们会将这次事故编码为需向警方报告的事故，因为撞击速度较高且更换安全气囊的成本较高——无论其他财产损失或伤害如何。

blocks.html_en: Using that common-sense standard, they estimated that only 22% of the 736 public-road crashes in which the automation was engaged were likely police-reportable — 89 crashes involving Waymo vehicles, 50 involving Cruise vehicles, 10 involving Zoox vehicles and 10 involving other companies. Because mileage numbers were only available for Waymo, they used its crashes to calculate self-driving vehicle crash rates. Of Waymo’s 89 police-reportable crash involvements, 64 were in driverless operation, and the others involved supervised use of the automation.

sentences.en: Using that common-sense standard, they estimated that only 22% of the 736 public-road crashes in which the automation was engaged were likely police-reportable — 89 crashes involving Waymo vehicles, 50 involving Cruise vehicles, 10 involving Zoox vehicles and 10 involving other companies.
sentences.zh: 根据这一常识标准，他们估计，在 736 起涉及自动化的公共道路事故中，只有 22% 可能需要向警方报告——其中 89 起事故涉及 Waymo 车辆，50 起涉及 Cruise 车辆，10 起涉及 Zoox 车辆，10 起涉及其他公司。

sentences.en: Because mileage numbers were only available for Waymo, they used its crashes to calculate self-driving vehicle crash rates.
sentences.zh: 由于里程数据仅适用于 Waymo，因此他们利用其碰撞事故来计算自动驾驶车辆的碰撞率。

sentences.en: Of Waymo’s 89 police-reportable crash involvements, 64 were in driverless operation, and the others involved supervised use of the automation.
sentences.zh: 在 Waymo 警方报告的 89 起事故中，有 64 起是无人驾驶操作，其他则涉及自动化操作的监督使用。

blocks.html_en: Waymo vehicles traveled about 50 million miles in driverless operation over the study period, compared with about 222 billion miles by human drivers over the same period and in the same locations. Overall, Waymo’s crash involvement rate was 68% lower than that of human drivers — 76% lower in Phoenix, 35% lower in San Francisco, 71% lower in Los Angeles, and 4% higher in Austin, where the sample size was relatively small. Waymo vehicles were involved in 85% fewer single-vehicle crashes and 81% fewer injury crashes per VMT than human drivers.

sentences.en: Waymo vehicles traveled about 50 million miles in driverless operation over the study period, compared with about 222 billion miles by human drivers over the same period and in the same locations.
sentences.zh: 在研究期间，Waymo 车辆在无人驾驶操作中行驶了约 5000 万英里，而同期人类驾驶员在相同地点行驶了约 2220 亿英里。

sentences.en: Overall, Waymo’s crash involvement rate was 68% lower than that of human drivers — 76% lower in Phoenix, 35% lower in San Francisco, 71% lower in Los Angeles, and 4% higher in Austin, where the sample size was relatively small.
sentences.zh: 总体而言，Waymo 的碰撞事故发生率比人类驾驶员低 68%，其中凤凰城低 76%，旧金山低 35%，洛杉矶低 71%，样本量相对较小的奥斯汀高 4%。

sentences.en: Waymo vehicles were involved in 85% fewer single-vehicle crashes and 81% fewer injury crashes per VMT than human drivers.
sentences.zh: 与人类驾驶员相比，Waymo 车辆发生的单车碰撞事故减少了 85%，每次 VMT 的伤害碰撞事故减少了 81%。

blocks.html_en: “Those are encouraging signs for the future of driverless vehicles,” said Eric Teoh, IIHS director of statistical services and lead author of the study. “Now we need to get the data collection system right, so that we can ensure that level of safety continues as these technologies become more prevalent.”

sentences.en: “Those are encouraging signs for the future of driverless vehicles,” said Eric Teoh, IIHS director of statistical services and lead author of the study.
sentences.zh: IIHS 统计服务主管、该研究的主要作者 Eric Teoh 表示：“这些都是无人驾驶汽车未来令人鼓舞的迹象。”

sentences.en: “Now we need to get the data collection system right, so that we can ensure that level of safety continues as these technologies become more prevalent.”
sentences.zh: “现在我们需要正确设置数据收集系统，这样我们就可以确保随着这些技术变得更加普遍，安全水平得以持续。”

content_html_zh:
新的 IIHS 研究显示，Waymo 的无人驾驶车辆的碰撞率比人类驾驶员低 68%。 但随着该技术的部署更加广泛，需要更好的安全跟踪系统。

July 23, 2026

公路安全保险研究所的一项新研究显示，Waymo 的无人驾驶汽车发生的事故比人类驾驶员少，但监控自动驾驶汽车的联邦系统需要改进。

研究人员发现，按照每车辆行驶里程 (VMT) 计算，Waymo 在旧金山、菲尼克斯、洛杉矶和奥斯汀部署的无人驾驶汽车发生的事故比人类驾驶员少 68%。 样本仅限于人们通常会向警方报告的事故。

IIHS 总裁戴维·哈基 (David Harkey) 表示：“结果表明，在有限的范围内，这些无人驾驶汽车比人类驾驶员更安全——人类驾驶员可能会受到损害、昏昏欲睡或注意力不集中。” “然而，目前的数据收集系统还不够好，无法持续监控大规模扩张。”

Waymo 于 2009 年作为 Google 自动驾驶汽车项目启动，于 2020 年在凤凰城向公众推出了无人驾驶机器人出租车服务，于 2022 年扩展到旧金山，于 2023 年扩展到洛杉矶，并于 2024 年开始在奥斯汀进行有限运营。 其车辆在SAE国际制定的自动化等级中被列为4级，这意味着在一定条件下，自动化可以完成所有驾驶，无需人工监督。

相比之下，公众广泛购买的二级部分自动化系统，例如通用汽车的 SuperCruise 或福特的 BlueCruise，需要人类驾驶员随时保持警惕并准备好接管。 这些系统涉及不同的风险：监管的单调性可能导致注意力疲劳，而且许多驾驶员对技术过于信任。

3 级系统可以在特定条件下在没有持续人工监督的情况下运行，但仍然需要有人坐在驾驶座上，并准备好在需要时进行干预。 配备此类系统的车辆非常罕见。 能够在所有条件下无需人工操作即可驾驶的 5 级车辆尚不存在。

自 2014 年以来，加州要求在公共道路上测试自动驾驶汽车的公司报告导致死亡、受伤或任何金额财产损失的事故。 美国国家公路交通安全管理局 (NHTSA) 于 2021 年开始根据一项关于涉及自动驾驶系统的事故报告的常规通令 (SGO) 要求提供类似的报告。

虽然 SGO 数据库的主要目的是跟踪缺陷和强制召回，但它是涉及自动驾驶车辆的碰撞事故的唯一国家数据收集工具。 因此，它是安全研究的重要资源。 然而，无论是它还是加州数据库都无法轻松地将 4 级车辆的碰撞率与人类驾驶员的碰撞率进行比较。

虽然公司必须报告事故，但他们没有义务报告他们记录的里程或这些里程是否涉及人类驾驶员。 Waymo 自愿提供无人驾驶 VMT 信息，但其他公司则不提供。 这使得我们无法知道他们的车辆发生碰撞的概率有多大。

IIHS 研究人员发现，即使使用 Waymo 提供的里程数据，如果不进行耗时的操作来解释 SGO 要求的报告与人类报告之间的差异，也不可能与人类的事故率进行直接比较。

人类驾驶员通常只需向警方报告造成人身伤害或造成 1,000 美元以上财产损失的碰撞事故，但损害阈值因州而异。 许多司机还选择不报告符合报告门槛的事故，以避免保险费增加或出于其他原因。 大约一半的事故和三分之一的伤害事故没有被报告。

相比之下，在 SGO 修正案于 2025 年生效之前，自动驾驶汽车公司必须将轻微事故记录为碰撞事故。 例如，车辆在驶入停车场时刮擦底盘将被视为碰撞事故。 尽管财产损失阈值现在更加一致，但由于不遵守法规的商业风险，公司仍然比普通人更有可能报告所有符合条件的事故。 此外，无人驾驶汽车需要昂贵的传感器，这些传感器可能会因轻微的撞击而损坏，从而使它们的碰撞更有可能超过损坏阈值。

因此，为了对事故率进行真实的比较，IIHS 研究人员必须清理 Cruise、Waymo、Zoox 等公司 2021 年至 2024 年向 NHTSA 提交的事故报告中的数据。 第一步是剔除数百条冗余记录。 他们还需要消除某些事件。 这些情况包括自动化未启用、车辆不在公共道路上或没有发生真正的碰撞事故的情况。 这一过程消除了大约四分之一的已报告崩溃。

下一步是确定事件是否严重到一个理智的人会向警方报告的程度。 研究人员根据影响和财产损失的性质、报告的伤害类型和其他因素做出了这一决定。 例如，如果一个人抱怨疼痛但离开了现场，研究人员会将这次事故编码为不太可能向警方报告。 如果安全气囊展开，他们会将这次事故编码为需向警方报告的事故，因为撞击速度较高且更换安全气囊的成本较高——无论其他财产损失或伤害如何。

根据这一常识标准，他们估计，在 736 起涉及自动化的公共道路事故中，只有 22% 可能需要向警方报告——其中 89 起事故涉及 Waymo 车辆，50 起涉及 Cruise 车辆，10 起涉及 Zoox 车辆，10 起涉及其他公司。 由于里程数据仅适用于 Waymo，因此他们利用其碰撞事故来计算自动驾驶车辆的碰撞率。 在 Waymo 警方报告的 89 起事故中，有 64 起是无人驾驶操作，其他则涉及自动化操作的监督使用。

在研究期间，Waymo 车辆在无人驾驶操作中行驶了约 5000 万英里，而同期人类驾驶员在相同地点行驶了约 2220 亿英里。 总体而言，Waymo 的碰撞事故发生率比人类驾驶员低 68%，其中凤凰城低 76%，旧金山低 35%，洛杉矶低 71%，样本量相对较小的奥斯汀高 4%。 与人类驾驶员相比，Waymo 车辆发生的单车碰撞事故减少了 85%，每次 VMT 的伤害碰撞事故减少了 81%。

IIHS 统计服务主管、该研究的主要作者 Eric Teoh 表示：“这些都是无人驾驶汽车未来令人鼓舞的迹象。” “现在我们需要正确设置数据收集系统，这样我们就可以确保随着这些技术变得更加普遍，安全水平得以持续。”
